description:
Second post-implementation phase of bl-f021. Beyond the per-ball conformance tests required by each implementation phase, this is the end-to-end sweep that exercises scenarios spanning the *whole* epic on fresh fixtures — no human-in-the-loop, no real claims, just CI-shaped runs that prove every documented path in SPEC-clone-layout actually works on a clean machine.

Depends on Phase 5 (bl-fb26): the dogfood run surfaces issues against the live dev environment; the integration sweep proves those issues are gone *and* covers cases the dev environment doesn't exercise (combined-history, moved-clone, master_url + XDG composition).

## Scope

### Migration matrix exercised

A fixture per starting state, each driven through every reachable migration path:

| Starting state | Driver | Expected end-state |
|---|---|---|
| Pre-XDG (`.balls/config.json` on main, `.balls/state-repo/`, colocated worktrees) | `bl migrate` | XDG three-file; one terminal commit on main; legacy paths gone |
| bl-ed32 two-file (`workspace.json` on state branch) | `bl migrate` | XDG three-file; one state-branch commit; no main commits |
| Combined-history (pre-XDG then bl-ed32 then nothing) | `bl migrate` | XDG three-file; no intermediate `workspace.json` ever written |
| Pre-XDG | `bl prime --migrate` | Same as row 1, invoked via prime |
| XDG (already-migrated) | `bl migrate` | No-op, exits 0 |

For each row: idempotence check (run twice; second is no-op) and dirty-tree refuse-check (uncommitted state aborts cleanly).

### Cross-version compatibility scenarios

- New `bl` reads pre-XDG clone: dual-read fallback fires; `bl prime` warns; `bl ready`/`bl claim` work normally.
- New `bl` reads bl-ed32 clone: same shape.
- Old `bl` (pre-Phase-1 binary) reads XDG clone: SPEC §12 documented-misroute behavior; `min_bl_version` warning fires per §14.24.
- Concurrent prime from two clones (one pre-migration, one post-migration) against shared hub: state-branch commits don't conflict; both complete.

### Federation + XDG composition

- `master_url` set on a post-migration clone: plugin policy lives on the hub per project_github_plugin memory; `.balls/state-repo/` content drives plugin dispatch; client-side `plugins` map ignored per the 'master wins' warning.
- Two clones of the same code repo on the same host, both XDG-migrated: per-`<path-hash>` isolation of claims/locks/worktrees holds; shared `<origin-key>` state-repo cache.
- Moved-clone scenario: rename the code repo's parent dir; `bl doctor` detects the orphaned `<path-hash>`; `bl repair --rebind-path` repairs it atomically.

### Tracker proxy bridge integration

Verify the bridge pattern (SKILL.md §'Bridging to an external tracker') still works post-XDG: one bridge clone with the github-issues plugin enabled, second clone proxy-only; balls filed from the proxy clone mirror outward via the bridge; tracker replies arrive at both clones.

## Exit criteria

- Every matrix row passes in CI (new integration test module).
- The cross-version scenarios pass against a checked-in old-`bl` fixture binary (or equivalent in-process simulation).
- The federation + bridge scenarios pass against a temp-dir hub fixture.

## Why a discrete ball

Per-ball conformance tests (§14.x) prove each implementation phase is correct in isolation. The integration sweep proves the *combination* is correct — and surfaces interaction bugs that only appear when two features touch each other (XDG × master_url is the obvious one). Bundling it onto any implementation phase would mix unit + integration scope and bloat the per-ball test count.

## Risks

- The old-`bl` fixture is awkward to maintain (it's a literally older binary). Alternative: a feature flag on current `bl` that disables dual-read code paths, simulating old-binary behavior. Decide during implementation; record the decision in this ball's notes.
title: Phase 6: full integration check sweep across migration matrix
